records.summary: Emulsion polymerization recipes usually depend on a small set of roles: monomers/comonomers, water or aqueous phase, surfactant package, initiator system, buffer or pH control, optional chain-transfer agent, and optional crosslinker.
guidance: Record reagent identity and physical properties in Master Reagents, then reference reagent_id from Formulations.
Keep monomer composition, surfactant level, initiator level, and feed profile explicit because they drive most interpretation.
Do not treat a recommendation as executable until SDS, SOP, and operating limits are reviewed.

records.summary: Particle size and colloidal stability are commonly affected by surfactant type/level, seed design, ionic strength, solids, agitation, and monomer feed rate.
guidance: When particle size is high, avoid changing monomer chemistry first; isolate surfactant package or feed duration.
When coagulum appears, capture where it formed, when it appeared, and whether it tracks feed, pH, temperature, or agitation.
Use DLS method details and sample dilution notes so changes are not instrument artifacts.
search_terms: emulsion polymerization surfactant particle size coagulum latex stability feed rate

records.summary: Initiator concentration, thermal profile, oxygen removal, and chase feed strategy influence conversion and residual monomer.
guidance: If conversion is low, verify initiator freshness, purge quality, and temperature before changing recipe composition.
Separate nucleation-stage initiator effects from chase-stage conversion effects in the Formulations tab.
Use the same conversion method across follow-up experiments.
search_terms: emulsion polymerization persulfate initiator conversion residual monomer oxygen inhibition

records.summary: Core-shell latex experiments need explicit core/shell ratio, core Tg target, shell composition, crosslinker level, and particle size target.
guidance: Do not interpret toughening performance without recording particle size, shell composition, and matrix compatibility.
Use staged formulation rows to distinguish core feed, shell feed, and chase additions.
search_terms: core shell latex acrylic impact modifier particle size Tg shell composition emulsion polymerization

records.summary: A useful lab notebook agent needs structured reagent identities, exact quantities, timestamps, observations, measurements, and links to evidence before it can recommend a defensible next run.
guidance: Make experiment_id and reagent_id stable because they are join keys across tabs.
Free-text observations are valuable, but measurements should be normalized into Results where possible.
Literature Evidence should cite DOI or URL and separate findings from interpretation.
search_terms: electronic lab notebook structured data experiment recommendation literature evidence
